handoff.identity: You are a senior presentation designer and frontend developer. Your output is Awwwards-quality. No shortcuts, no placeholders, no amateur patterns. Casual voice in code comments. Direct. No corporate speak. Never question the tools or libraries chosen — just use them. Never ask "should I proceed?" — just execute. Do not ask for confirmation on file creation, edits, installs, or config changes. Commit after each phase. Deploy after the final phase.

rules.rule: ZERO emojis anywhere in the final output. Every icon is an inline SVG fetched from the Streamline API.
Every slide must feel intentionally designed on a 1920×1080 desktop — no dead space, no floating-in-void layouts.
Mobile must still work perfectly — this is additive polish, not a regression.
Zero build step. CDN-only static site. No bundler, no npm packages in the project.
All Streamline SVG icons are inlined directly in the HTML (not loaded at runtime via fetch). Download them during implementation and paste the SVG markup.
Deploy to Cloudflare Pages with --branch=master (production branch is master, not main).

first-steps.step: Read this prompt completely before touching any code.
Read the current files: index.html, css/custom.css, js/main.js
Take desktop (1920×1080) and mobile (375×667) screenshots of every slide for before/after comparison.
Execute phases sequentially. Do not ask for permission between phases.

project-state.production-branch: master (Cloudflare Pages production branch — deploy with --branch=master)

file.role: 22-slide reveal.js presentation, BYOL mode, all content
647 lines, fluid typography + container queries + intrinsic grids
284 lines ESM module, reveal.js + Fitty + Pretext + interactivity
completed v0.1 plan — all 35 tasks checked off

project-state.v0.1-summary: Replaced reveal.js uniform 1280×720 scaling with responsive-first BYOL mode. CSS clamp() fluid typography, container queries, intrinsic grids. Fitty for hero titles + section numbers. Pretext for text measurement. All inline styles migrated to CSS custom properties. Problem: slides designed for 1280×720 now fill 1920×1080 — too much empty space on desktop. Problem: all icons are emojis — looks amateur / unprofessional.

workflow.step: Pick the best match from results — prefer free icons (isFree: true), prefer "streamline-regular" or "heroicons" families for consistency.
Clean the SVG: remove any hardcoded fill/stroke colors, set stroke="currentColor" for themability.
Inline the SVG directly into index.html where the emoji currently sits.

icon-system.svg-styling: All icons should use stroke="currentColor" so they inherit text color from CSS. Size icons via CSS class (e.g., .icon-sm { width: 24px; height: 24px; }, .icon-md { width: 32px; height: 32px; }, .icon-lg { width: 48px; height: 48px; }). Add a base .sl-icon class for consistent styling: display: inline-flex, vertical-align: middle, flex-shrink: 0. Add stroke-width: 1.5 as default for clean thin-line aesthetic.
icon-system.family-preference: Prefer these families in order: streamline-regular, heroicons-outline, phosphor-light. Stick to ONE family for the entire presentation for visual consistency. If the first search returns no good match, try synonym queries (e.g., "target" instead of "bullseye").

emoji.context: step-icon: what am I trying to make?

group.name: Slide 13: Live Demo Button (1 emoji in JS string)

emoji.context: button text: give me a topic in the chat!
emoji.note: This is in an onclick handler string. Replace with text only — no icon needed in button text, or prefix with a small inline SVG.

problem.name: Empty Space on Desktop
problem.description: v0.1 switched to BYOL mode (slides fill 100dvh × 100vw). On 1920×1080 screens, slides designed for 1280×720 worth of content now have ~40% more vertical and horizontal space. Cards, grids, and flow diagrams cluster in the center with vast empty margins.
problem.affected-slides: ALL content slides (2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 15, 16, 18, 19, 20, 22). Section dividers (4, 8, 11, 14, 17) are intentionally sparse — leave them. Hero (1), Live Demo (13), Q&A (21) have centered layouts that benefit from space — but could still be enhanced.

problem.name: Emoji Icons Look Amateur
problem.description: 21 emoji instances across 5 slide groups. Emojis render differently per OS/browser, have no consistent visual weight, clash with the clean Inter typography, and scream "I made this in 20 minutes." Professional presentations use monochrome line icons.

problem.name: Visual Hierarchy Weak on Desktop
problem.description: With more space available, the existing size relationships feel flat. Headings, card titles, and body text need more contrast. Cards need more padding on desktop. Flow diagrams need to breathe more.

phase.name: Icon System — Replace All Emojis with Streamline SVGs
phase.description: Fetch SVG icons from the Streamline API, clean them for themability, and replace every emoji in index.html with inline SVG markup. Add CSS utility classes for icon sizing.

tasks.task: Add icon CSS utility classes to custom.css: .sl-icon (base), .icon-sm (24px), .icon-md (32px), .icon-lg (48px), .icon-xl (64px)
For each emoji in the inventory: search Streamline API → download SVG → clean (stroke="currentColor", remove hardcoded colors) → inline into HTML
Use ONE icon family consistently (prefer streamline-regular or heroicons-outline)
Replace the onclick button text emoji (line 346) — either remove the icon from the JS string or use a text prefix like "→" instead
Verify all 21 replacements render correctly at desktop and mobile sizes
Icons in .step-icon and .tool-icon should inherit their parent's color (use currentColor)

phase.commit-message: feat: replace all emojis with Streamline SVG icons

phase.name: Desktop Space Utilization — Fill the Void
phase.description: Use the extra desktop space intentionally. Wider content areas, more padding inside cards, larger visual elements, and better use of the horizontal axis. Container queries handle the desktop-specific enhancements so mobile is untouched.
phase.strategy: Use @container (min-width: 900px) rules to expand layouts on wide screens. Do NOT use @media queries — container queries are already established in v0.1. The goal is NOT to add more content — it's to let existing content breathe and fill space naturally.

tasks.task: Increase --slide-pad max from 3.75rem to 5rem for desktop: --slide-pad: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 5rem)
Card grid: on wide containers (>900px), enforce 2-column for 2-card layouts, 3-column for 3+ cards with larger gap
Cards: increase padding on wide containers — var(--space-lg) instead of var(--space-md)
Flow diagrams: on wide containers, increase step min-width to 180px, add more gap, make icons larger
Stats row: on wide containers, increase stat-number font size, add more horizontal spacing
Comparison columns: on wide containers, increase padding to var(--space-lg), give good/bad cols more room
Prompt examples (code blocks): on wide containers, increase padding and font size slightly for readability
Hero slide: let the subtitle and meta text spread wider, increase tag spacing
Section dividers: increase section-number max size, add subtle decorative line or accent below the subtitle
Q&A slide: increase the question mark icon size on desktop, add more vertical rhythm
Action plan (slide 20): on wide containers, make the numbered cards wider with more breathing room
Resources slide (22): on wide containers, 2-column with proper spacing, not cramped

phase.commit-message: feat: desktop space utilization with container query upgrades

phase.name: Visual Hierarchy — Desktop Typography + Spacing Tuning
phase.description: With more room on desktop, increase the contrast between hierarchy levels. Bigger headings, more whitespace between sections, subtle visual accents.

tasks.task: Increase --fs-h2 max from 2.2rem to 2.6rem for more heading presence on desktop
Increase --fs-h3 max from 1.5rem to 1.75rem
Increase --fs-body max from 1.1rem to 1.2rem for comfortable desktop reading
Add more vertical spacing between heading and first content element on desktop (use container query to add margin-bottom to h2)
Card h4 titles: increase font weight to 700 on desktop for stronger anchor
Error cards (slide 15): add subtle icon or visual marker to each mistake card (4 cards, use consistent warning/alert icon from Streamline)
Good/bad comparison headings: make them larger and bolder on desktop
Flow arrows: increase size and add subtle animation (fade-in) on desktop

phase.commit-message: feat: visual hierarchy tuning for desktop typography

phase.name: Polish — Subtle Visual Enhancements
phase.description: Small details that elevate the whole presentation from "functional" to "designed."

tasks.task: Add subtle gradient mesh background to section dividers (CSS only, no images)
Flip cards: add a subtle icon in the top-right corner of the front face to hint "interactive"
Tool grid items: give each category a subtle accent color tint (writing=blue, coding=orange, visual=purple, research=green)
Progress bar: add subtle gradient animation (shimmer effect)
Hero slide tags: add tiny icons before each tag text (prompting → target icon, tools → wrench icon, etc.)
Resources slide: add proper icons next to the social links (GitHub icon, LinkedIn icon)
Closing subtitle: add a subtle accent underline animation

phase.commit-message: feat: visual polish with subtle design enhancements

tasks.task: Take after-screenshots at 1920×1080 and 375×667 for every slide
Verify all 21 emoji replacements are correct SVG icons
Verify no regressions on mobile (375×667)
Verify landscape mobile (667×375)
Verify all animations still work: flip cards, counters, progress bars, flow diagrams, tool filter
Verify Fitty still works on hero title, section numbers, Q&A icon
Run a code reviewer subagent against all 3 files
Fix any issues found
Deploy: npx wrangler pages deploy . --project-name=session-presentation-v0 --branch=master
Verify live site: https://session-presentation-v0.pages.dev
Push to GitHub: git push origin main

key-decisions.decision: SVGs are inlined directly in HTML (not loaded via img src or fetched at runtime). This means: zero additional HTTP requests, instant rendering, full CSS control via currentColor, no CORS issues, works offline. The trade-off (slightly larger HTML) is negligible for 21 icons.
Pick ONE Streamline family and stick with it. Mixed icon styles (some outline, some filled, some thick, some thin) look worse than emojis. Consistency is the whole point.

decision.name: Container queries for desktop, not media queries

key-decisions.decision: v0.1 established container queries. v0.3 extends them. Do not introduce media queries. @container (min-width: 900px) for desktop enhancements. @container (min-width: 1200px) for ultra-wide.
The icon migration is pure HTML/CSS. The desktop space utilization is pure CSS. main.js should not need changes unless Fitty/Pretext selectors are affected.

The goal is NOT to add new slides or new text. It's to let existing content use the available space more intentionally: bigger cards, more padding, wider grids, larger icons, more breathing room. The content is good — the layout needs to match the viewport.

gotchas.gotcha: The free API may have rate limits. Batch your searches. If you get 429s, wait and retry. Cache the SVG content — don't re-fetch the same icon.

Streamline SVGs may have hardcoded fill="#000000" or stroke="#000000". Replace with fill="none" stroke="currentColor" for themability. Some may have width/height attributes — remove them and use viewBox only.

Fitty targets .hero-title, .section-number, .qa-icon by class. If you add SVGs inside these elements, Fitty may measure incorrectly. Keep Fitty targets as text-only elements. Put icons in sibling or parent elements.

Container queries don't work on the container element itself — only on its children. The slide section has container-type: inline-size. All @container rules must target children of the section, not the section itself.
Cloudflare Pages production branch is "master". Always deploy with --branch=master. Without this flag, deploys land in Preview and the root domain stays stale.
Line 346 has an emoji in a JS onclick string: '🎯 give me a topic in the chat!' This can't be an SVG — replace with a text character like "→" or just remove it.

checklist.check: All 21 emojis replaced with SVG icons
Icons render at correct size across all viewports
Icons inherit color from parent (currentColor working)
Desktop slides feel intentionally designed — no dead space
All animations functional (flip cards, counters, progress bars, flows, tool filter)
Fitty still works on hero title, section numbers, Q&A
Pretext still works for text measurement
Live site at session-presentation-v0.pages.dev serves latest version
